texts.full_text: role . To fully answer this question one must examine the identity of the Hong Kongnese people, who they are and who they identify with most. To me the Hong Kongnese identity can be compared to that of an adopted child that is taken from its biological mother at a very young age and raised by a another mother. The child’s uniqueness, charisma and beauty is a combination of its upbringing (thanks to the foreign mother) and its natural charisma (thanks to the biological mother.) You cannot isolate either side to describe her. A grown up now, she is trying to get used to her birth mother’s ways and realizes that their newly found co-existence is much harder than she had expected. Behind Hong Kong’s uniqueness there are some interesting facts . Here are few that culturally underlie the debate about Hong Kong: Hong Kong was Chinese territory taken by the British. In iraq, the violence has declined from peak levels, but it actually started dropping even before the U.S. troop surge, primarily because severe ethnic cleansing had separated the warring Sunnis and Shia into homogenous ghettos, and because the United States had begun to pay off the Sunni guerrillas to police their local areas and fight the excessively bloodthirsty (and therefore incompetent) al-Qaeda in Iraq. More important, evidence exists that the militias in Iraq, like all good guerrilla forces, have patience and are merely waiting until the United States leaves. Even with the surge, violence – although reduced – is still high, and no national reconciliation among the mutually suspicious groups has been achieved. And it’s likely that none will be. The selection includes many of Millay's most famous works, as well as lesser-known pieces that showcase her range and skill. Millay's poetry is marked by a keen observation of the natural world, a deep emotional intensity, and a distinctive musicality. Her themes often revolve around love, loss, and the passage of time. In "Renascence," she explores the idea of rebirth and renewal, while in "What Lips My Lips Have Kissed," she reflects on the fleeting nature of love and memory. One of the strengths of this collection is its diversity. Millay's work spans from sonnets to villanelles, from free verse to blank verse. She also experimented with different forms and styles throughout her career, demonstrating a remarkable versatility. However, the collection also highlights some of the challenges of reading Millay's poetry today. Her language can sometimes feel dated, and her references may be lost on modern readers. FRONT MATTER CHAPTER 10 CHAPTER 9 CHAPTER 8 CHAPTER 7 CHAPTER 6 CHAPTER 5 CHAPTER 4 CHAPTER 3 CHAPTER 2 CHAPTER 1 BACK MATTER CHAPTER 0 Blog Glossary 9.IV: The Sexual Evolution The original “tree of life” model of speciation from Charles Darwin’s notebook, 1837. The science of life’s early history is much like the study of the early universe. Abiogenesis itself, the initial emergence of life from ocean chemistry, was a singular event that occurred about four billion years ago on our planet Earth. As we delve into this chapter titled "9.IV: The Sexual Evolution," it's crucial to understand how sexual reproduction came to be and its profound impact not only in shaping the diversity of life but also influencing evolutionary processes. Darwin’s depiction, often referred as a 'tree' model for speciation (the process by which new distinct species evolve from pre-existing ones), is an iconic representation that has been instrumental since his time. The concept suggests branching patterns where each lineage diverges into another over generations due to accumulated genetic differences. This mechanism of evolution through natural selection, as Darwin proposed in "On the Origin of Species," was revolutionary and fundamentally changed our understanding about life's diversity on Earth—a process driven by competition for resources among individuals with different traits. However, it is important not only consider how species evolve but also understand that sexual reproduction itself evolved. The transition from asexual to sex-based reproductive strategies has been one the most significant evolutionary developments in biological history and continues shaping our world today through mechanisms like genetic recombination which increase variation within populations—a key factor for survival. In this chapter, we explore these concepts further: how life's diversity is generated by sexual reproduction; what are its advantages over asexual methods of propagation (such as binary fission or budding); and the role it plays in driving evolution. We will also discuss some theories on why sex evolved at all—a question that has intrigued scientists for

texts.full_text: nd severity of OHSS, pregnancy rates per cycle started, live birth rate per egg retrieval attempt, clinical pregnancies achieved, miscarriage rates, gestation occurrence, patient satisfaction, treatment cost-effectiveness, ovarian recovery time post-treatment, and any adverse events associated with the interventions. ### Results: Several studies have shown that embryo cryopreservation can be an effective strategy for preventing OHSS. One major advantage is its ability to delay or avoid fresh transfer in women at high risk of developing severe OHSS following hCG administration (triggering ovulation). By freezing all embryos, clinicians are able to mitigate the fluid shift associated with egg retrieval and subsequent embryo development without compromising fertility outcomes. Comparatively, human intra-venous albumin infusion has been used as a treatment for mild cases or prevention in high-risk patients. While it can be effective at managing symptoms of OHSS by increasing plasma volume, its efficacy is generally considered inferior to that of cryopreservation when severe forms are concerned and may not entirely prevent the development of clinical manifestations. ### Conclusion: Embryo freezing appears more promising than human intra-venous albumin infusion for preventing or treating moderate-to-severe cases of ovarian hyperstimulation syndrome. It allows better control over treatment timing, reduces complications related to fluid shifts during pregnancy stages after superovulation and offers fertility outcomes with fewer risks associated with OHSS. However, the decision between these two strategies should be individualized based on patient-specific factors such as age, egg quality, ovarian response history, clinical stage of hyperstimulation syndrome if present at retrieval time among others. Further research is warranted to confirm long-term safety and efficacy profiles across diverse populations undergoing reproductive technologies involving superovulatory protocols. texts.full_text: is where this was to end, with me telling you, that I cannot tell you the ending. That is, until a recent serendipitous encounter. An hour’s drive from where I live now, there sits a typical English seaside town, Southport, which is neither in the south or a port. My father was born there. A Sandgrounder. The town boasts a fine Victorian art gallery and museum, The Atkinson, and it was here, while strolling through the newly refurbished galleries, that I chanced upon a small, dimly lit display case. Inside was an old, rather faded, top hat. I glanced at the caption. Top Hat belonging to P.T. Barnum. Photo courtesy of the Atkinson I was like a kid at christmas. My wife came over to see what the fuss was about, and together we read on. It seems P.T.Barnum was an advisor to the Botanic Gardens Mueum, which opened in Southport in 1876. texts.full_text: s but renal involvement is rare. This report is of a case of Kawasaki disease complicated by acute kidney failure. A 10 year-old girl was admitted because of acute renal failure with fever. She developed a high fever, and her general condition was poor; she had developed a macular erythematous rash 10 days earlier for which she was given cefadroxil. At admission, she remained febrile and had strawberry tongue, pharyngitis, dry erythematous lips, bilateral conjunctivitis, cervical lymphadenopathy and desquamation of the skin on her hands. She was anemic (hemoglobin = 9.6 g%), leukocytotic (33,100/mm3), but with no burr, fragmented red blood cells or thrombocytopenia. Her plasma C-reactive protein level was 236 mg/l; her blood urea was 9.5 mmol/l, her creatininemia 288 mumol/l and proteinuria was 0.5 g/l without hematuria. Urine cultures did not grow. Her blood transaminase and gammaglutamyltransferase activities were elevated. Ultrasonography of kidneys and coronary arteries was normal. texts.full_text: of this study ======================================= - The first study to be carried out among this sector. - Single-Summary: The article highlights the significant risk of musculoskeletal disorders (MSDs) among manual solid waste collectors. Here's a concise summary: Focus: - Population: Manual municipal solid waste (MSW) collectors. - Issue: High risk of musculoskeletal disorders (MSDs). Key Findings: 1. MSDs are highly prevalent among waste collectors due to the physically demanding nature of their job. 2. Risk factors contributing to MSDs can be categorized into: - Personal factors: Age, gender, smoking, etc. - Psychosocial factors: Job satisfaction, work stress, etc. - Ergonomic factors: Heavy lifting, awkward postures, repetitive movements, etc. 3. Mechanization of waste collection could significantly reduce the risk of MSDs by minimizing heavy lifting and other ergonomic stressors. Strengths & Limitations: - Strength: This is the first study to focus on this specific occupational sector, highlighting the need for further research and intervention. - Limitation: As a single study, its findings may not be generalizable to all waste collection settings or populations. texts.full_text: men waited patiently to hear if they had been awarded the coveted Heisman Trophy. Since my wife is an avid Arkansas Razorback fan, there was a desire to see Darren McFadden, who came in second last year, win the award this year. We know that due to the coaching changes at Arkansas and his professional playing prospects, he will not be playing college ball next season, so this was his last chance. Chase Daniel of Missouri and Colt Brennan of Hawaii were there also, but everyone knew they were just along for the ride. The story was Tim Tebow, the sophomore quarterback from the University of Florida. He's been called Superman by the media all season, and after watching him play and looking at his stats, it's easy to see why. He's truly an incredible football player. Since the guidelines for the Heisman are that the "most outstanding college player" is to receive it, he fulfilled the criteria. The only caveat was that he is a sophomore. No underclassman had ever won the award until last night. Then, the moment happened. Tim Tebow's name was read. His speech focused on his priorities in life: Christ, family, academics and football. His sincerity was obvious and I think he named and thanked everyone he's ever met. It was a good moment. I'm not a Gator fan, though I'm not "anti-Gator" either. I like the SEC and was proud that two of our guys were there. After it was all said and done, I felt bad for McFadden, but I imagine he'll be OK once he signs his NFL signing

texts.full_text: ploring why Germany is referred to as the "virtue country," it's essential to delve into the historical and cultural contexts of naming conventions across different languages. The term "德" in Mandarin Chinese, which translates to "Virtue," reflects a deep-rooted appreciation for German values such as discipline, precision, and respect for tradition. This perception is mirrored in Vietnamese with the term "đệ nhất," underscoring a similar admiration for Germany's ethical standards and commitment to excellence. Contrastingly, the Japanese name for Germany, derived from the Dutch adjective "Duitsland," carries a different connotation. Historically, this naming pattern reflects Japan's historical interactions with the Netherlands during the nineteenth century, influencing their perspective on German culture and societal norms. The flattering connotations of the Chinese characters contrast sharply with those in Japanese, showcasing how cultural perceptions and historical contexts significantly shape naming patterns and linguistic translations across different languages. This analysis underscores the importance of understanding these nuances to appreciate the diverse ways countries are perceived globally. Noticeably, the characters with which the Chinese name is written have a flattering connotation while the Japanese characters are degrading. This is consistent with naming patterns of the two countries during the nineteenth century. The Korean name is based on the Japanese name. texts.full_text: Activator for Traumatic Retained Hemothorax. Objective: To describe the efficacy, safety, dosing regimen, and administration technique of intrapleural alteplase for the treatment of retained hemothorax. Data Sources: A PubMed, EMBASE, and Google Scholar search (January 2000 to February 2019) was conducted with the search terms intrapleural, fibrinolytic, fibrinolysis, alteplase, tissue plasminogen activator, and hemothorax. Study Selection and Data Extraction: Articles were included if they described the use of intrapleural alteplase in adult patients with a retained hemothorax; single patient case reports and abstracts were excluded. Data Synthesis: A total of 6 retrospective reviews and 1 meta-analysis were identified for inclusion. A variety of dosing strategies have been defined for the administration of intrapleural alteplase ranging from 6 to 100 mg, volume of fluid from 50 to 120 mL of normal saline, and the number of total doses has ranged from 1 to 8 over the treatment course. A majority of studies showed a greater than 80% success rate and less than 7% bleeding rate. It was reconstructed on a plot of ground less than a quarter of a mile from the original site. We offer events and programs highlighting several hundred years of history. Our goal is to educate the community about the Old Fort and its significance to the Old Northwest Territory, the State of Indiana, and the United States during the 17th and 18th centuries. When in the citWhen in the citadel, visitors are transported back to the early 19th century, experiencing the life of the men and women who served in the fort and the surrounding communities. The Old Fort serves not only as a historical site but as a dynamic educational and cultural resource. texts.full_text: n 18% and 17% respectively. Forty-nine patients (42%) presented with limited-stage disease and 67 (58%) with extensive-stage disease. Thirty patients (26%) received chemotherapy alone, 23 patients (20%) received radiotherapy alone, 33 patients (28%) received combined chemoradiotherapy, and 30 patients (26%) received supportive treatment. A chemotherapy regimen of cisplatin combined with etoposide was used in 61 of 63 patients (97%). The response to chemotherapy was complete remission in 12 cases (19%), and partial response in 20 cases (32%). The median survival of limited-stage disease was significantly better than those with extensive-stage disease (44 weeks vs. 22 weeks). Patients with chemotherapy treatment had significantly improved median survival in both limited-stage and extensive-stage disease.
